rulings.Case Title: BRUCE GRAHAM VS. TOTAL FAMILY SUPPORT CLINIC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions, Or Alternatively, Order For Deemed Admissions And Order Compelling Responses To Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 30, 2010, line 2. PLAINTIFF BRUCE GRAHAM'S Motion For Terminating Sanctions, Or Alternatively, Order For Deemed Admissions And Order Compelling Responses To Discovery. Motion for terminating sanctions is GRANTED, NO OPPOSITION FILED. Defendants' answer is striken and a default judgment shall be entered. Plaintiff is awarded sanctions in the amount of $2,112.50 to be paid by defendants within 30 days of notice of this order. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: STACIE IVERY et al VS. WALGREEN COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Appearance For Oral Deposition And Production Of Documents And Request For Monetary Sanctions; Declarations Of William T. Mulvihill, Jacquelyn S. Lewis And Jo Custer Melnyk, Esq. In Support
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 30, 2010, line 4. DEFENDANT WALGREEN CO., IMPROPERLY SUED HEREIN AS WALGREEN'S Motion To Compel Appearance For Oral Deposition And Production Of Documents And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Granted in part. If the trial date is not continued, plaintiff to appear for deposition by 1/09/11 to be scheduled on nights or weekends. If trial continued, plaintiff to appear for deposition within 2 weeks of expiration of probationary period on a work weekday. Requests for sanctions denied, both parties acted within substantial justification. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: STACIE IVERY et al VS. WALGREEN COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Omtion For Reconsideration Of The Order Granting Walgreen Co'S Motion To Compel Pltf'S Further Discovery Responses And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 30, 2010, line 4. LAINTIFFS STACIE IVERY and JEROME IVERY'S Motion For Reconsideration Of The Order Granting Walgreen Co'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Further Discovery Responses And Request For Monetary Sanctions. DENIED. Providing unverified responses does not render the motion moot. The desire to present oral argument is not a new fact that warrants reconsideration. See Gilberd v. AC Transit (1995) 32 Cal. App. 4th. 1494, 1500. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: STACIE IVERY et al VS. WALGREEN COMPANY et al

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 30, 2010, line 4. DEFENDANT WALGREEN CO., IMPROPERLY SUED HEREIN AS WALGREEN'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff's Responses To Discovery, And Request For Monetary Sanctions. GRANTED, NO OPPOSITION FILED. Defendant is awarded $560 sanctions. The court adopts defendants proposed order. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: SONYA BRYANT et al VS. SOUTHWESTERN BELL YELLOW PAGES, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 30, 2010, line 1. DEFENDANT PACIFIC BELL DIRECTORY (SUED AS SOUTHWESTERN BELL)'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for Summary judgment Denied. Motion for summary adjudication granted as to punitive damages claims. Plaintiffs present no evidence that any officer, director, or managing agent of defendant either engaged in, authorized, or ratified any allegedly wrongful conduct. CC 3294(b). Summary adjudication denied in all other respects. Triable issues of fact raised regarding discrimination and pretext: Plintiffs' non-African American male counterparts failed to reach the numbers of the performance plan, yet were not placed on PIPS and did not have key accounts removed, but received key accounts taken from plaintiffs; those counterparts performances were cured by the ad-hoc application of mitigators. However, when plaintiffs requested mitigators they were denied. The performance paln was a sham because no writing setting it forth was given to plaintiffs by their supervisors, and the performance plan was never "rolled out." Triable issues of fact raised regarding retaliation: Complaints were filed by plaintiffs both internally and externally including the early EEO complaint against Sample and Nantria, informal complaints of discrimination in 12/08. EEO complaintis about Nett, complaints of discrimination and retaliation with CA Department of Fair Employment and Housing and right to sue letters of 10/2/09. Retaliation raised in suspension of Pete without pay in 2/09, not getting back key accounts after being taken off the PIP, not having mitigators applied, and remaining on PIPs. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JEFFREY WAYMAN VS. STEPHEN WALKER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion For Order Compelling Responses To Discovery And Awarding Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 30, 2010, line 3. PLAINTIFF JEFFREY WAYMAN'S Motion For Order Compelling Responses To Discovery And Awarding Sanctions. Denied as to defendants. The case is stayed pursuant to the filing of bankruptcy. Sanctions denied as to counsel. It would be unjust to sanction counsel who advised his clients of their discovery obligations, and where the clients neither provided the necessary information nor funded the legal works in connection with it. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: OGDEN CONTRACT INTERIORS, INC., A CALIFORNIA VS. VAN NESS TOWERS, LLC, A LIMITED LIABILITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Expunge Mechanic'S Lien And Request For Attorneys' Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 30, 2010, line 7. DEFENDANTS VAN NESS TOWERS, LLC. and VAN NESS AND TURK PROPERTIES, LLC.'S Motion To Expunge Mechanic's Lien And Request For Attorneys' Fees. Motion to expunge the mechanics lien is granted. Plaintiff's recordation of the lien was untimely under CC 3115. Request for attorney fees is denied without prejudice. =(302/CWW)
